label: Commodity Regime
description: Regime detection dashboard with factor scores, trading signals, and active commodity positions with P&L.

0.5.note: no lib/mocks imports — data via useStrategiesData().

0.6.note: explicit isLoading guard renders loading card.

0.7.note: explicit empty guard (factors.length===0 && positions.length===0) renders empty card.

0.8.note: — context does not expose error field; WidgetErrorBoundary covers thrown exceptions; context falls back to mock data silently on API error.

l0.notes: 0.1 ✓ WidgetErrorBoundary in widget-wrapper. 0.2 ✓ requiredEntitlements=[execution-basic,execution-full]. 0.3 ✓ availableOn=[strategies,overview]. 0.4 ✓ no any/ts-ignore. 0.5 ✓ no lib/mocks imports — data via useStrategiesData(). 0.6 ✓ explicit isLoading guard renders loading card. 0.7 ✓ explicit empty guard (factors.length===0 && positions.length===0) renders empty card. 0.8 NOTED — context does not expose error field; WidgetErrorBoundary covers thrown exceptions; context falls back to mock data silently on API error. 0.9 ✓ no dead code.

1.1.note: commodityRegime sourced exclusively from useStrategiesData() hook.

1.2.note: context calls useGlobalScope(). 1.3-1.6 NOTE — commodity regime data (factors, positions) is domain-specific mock data not filtered by org/client/strategy scope; it represents market-wide regime signals rather than per-client positions. Scope filtering applies to the strategies list; commodity data is intentionally global.

1.3.note: 1.6 NOTE — commodity regime data (factors, positions) is domain-specific mock data not filtered by org/client/strategy scope; it represents market-wide regime signals rather than per-client positions. Scope filtering applies to the strategies list; commodity data is intentionally global.

l1.notes: 1.1 ✓ commodityRegime sourced exclusively from useStrategiesData() hook. 1.2 ✓ context calls useGlobalScope(). 1.3-1.6 NOTE — commodity regime data (factors, positions) is domain-specific mock data not filtered by org/client/strategy scope; it represents market-wide regime signals rather than per-client positions. Scope filtering applies to the strategies list; commodity data is intentionally global. 1.7 ✓ MOCK_COMMODITY_REGIME lives in strategies-data-context.tsx (not in lib/mocks/).

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab (structured {domain, tier} entitlements across all 17 register.ts files + 5 PageEntitlementGate wrappers) and f9f29ea (65 automated tests in __tests__/integration/entitlement-wiring.test.ts, __tests__/components/platform/*entitlement-gate.test.tsx, __tests__/lib/config/trading-entitlements.test.ts). Re-audit per-widget only if the wiring test fails or entitlements are added.

findings: 0.8 NOTE: strategies-data-context does not surface API error field. WidgetErrorBoundary covers thrown exceptions.

1.3-1.6 NOTE: commodity regime data (currentRegime, factors, positions) is market-wide signal data — not scoped per org/client. This is architecturally correct; scope filtering applies to the strategy list, not regime indicators.
Previously known issue resolved: inline MOCK_FACTORS and MOCK_POSITIONS removed from widget file; data now sourced from strategies-data-context via useStrategiesData().
